topic: Real tea-rags MCP calls verifying that the skill's claims (typed sugar enums, level=file enforcement, picomatch negation) are actually accepted by the server, and that the failure modes the skill prevents (testFile boolean) are actually rejected.

cases.claim: testFile: "only" is accepted by schema as enum string
cases.call: semantic_search(project: tea-rags, query: "reranker", testFile: "only", limit: 3, metaOnly: true)
cases.expected: Schema accepts; response is a valid SearchResponse (results may be empty)

cases.claim: level: "file" + minAgeDays: 30 enforces file-level time filter
cases.expected: Response includes level: "file" echo and only files with ageDays >= 30
cases.observed: 5 results, response field level: "file", all results with git.file.ageDays in [31, 33, 34, 39] — all >= 30
cases.skill_claim_verified: level=file enforcement for time-based filters works

cases.claim: pathPattern picomatch negation !**/test/** excludes test subtree
cases.call: semantic_search(query: "reranker scoring", pathPattern: "!**/test/**", limit: 5, metaOnly: true)
cases.observed: 5 results — all paths in website/docs/, src/core/, none under /test/
cases.skill_claim_verified: Picomatch negation in pathPattern works as a typed-sugar-equivalent for directory exclusion

cases.claim: testFile: true (boolean) is REJECTED by schema — this is the baseline failure mode the skill prevents
cases.expected: MCP schema error — invalid enum, expected 'only'|'exclude'|'include'
cases.observed: MCP error -32602: "Input validation error: ... Invalid option: expected one of \"only\"|\"exclude\"|\"include\""
cases.skill_claim_verified: Boolean testFile is rejected by the live server — Phase 2 baseline subagents that proposed testFile: true/false would have failed in production. Skill's enforcement of enum strings is CRITICAL for end-to-end correctness.

cases.claim: taskId typed sugar is accepted by schema
cases.expected: Schema accepts; response valid (RAGS-142 is fictitious — empty results expected)
cases.observed: { results: [] } — schema accepted, no matching task ids (as expected)

verdict: All 5 Phase 8 cases passed. Critical finding: F8-4 confirms that the schema rejects boolean testFile — the most common baseline failure mode (4/10 baseline cases in Phase 2 trigger coverage) would fail in production with MCP error -32602. The skill's enforcement of enum strings is not stylistic; it is required for the call to succeed.
